jokes.text: Always scare Danielson, Miyagi hate fighting.
jokes.setup: Chang practicing lines from Karate Kid
jokes.punchline: Mangling the iconic quote with poor grammar/pronunciation
jokes.explanation: Establishes Chang's characteristic incompetence at even basic memorization, setting up the episode's theater storyline

jokes.text: You got a problem with that, racist?
jokes.setup: Question about Chang auditioning for Daniel LaRusso
jokes.punchline: Chang immediately playing the race card defensively
jokes.explanation: Pure Chang - immediately going to extremes and making everything about persecution

jokes.text: They took my snake, and they turned it into a plumber. They made the eggs, into barrels. The pine tree they made into a gorilla, but I recognized my design. Donkey Kong my ass. That's construction snake.
jokes.setup: Elroy apparently claiming to have invented video game concepts
jokes.punchline: Insisting Donkey Kong stole his 'construction snake' design
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character establishment for Elroy - the delusional inventor who sees his ideas everywhere. 'Construction snake' is memorably absurd.

jokes.text: Why would a tree throw eggs at a snake? / Why would a papal be fighting a monkey?
jokes.setup: Trying to make sense of Elroy's video game conspiracy
jokes.punchline: Abed's 'papal' mispronunciation turning plumber Mario into the Pope
jokes.explanation: Nice wordplay moment where Abed's mishearing creates an even more absurd image

jokes.text: What happened to good morning? / It's a bad morning, no Wi-Fi.
jokes.setup: Dean commenting on lack of pleasantries
jokes.punchline: Jeff's blunt dismissal that no Wi-Fi ruins everything
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment showing Jeff's priorities and the group's Wi-Fi dependency

jokes.text: Okay, let's not get carried away. We need oxygen. / We have oxygen, we need Wi-Fi.
jokes.setup: Dean trying to put Wi-Fi in perspective with basic needs
jokes.punchline: Jeff treating Wi-Fi as equally essential to breathing
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure and captures modern dependency on internet connectivity

jokes.text: My emails to her get bounced back to me in Aramaic, and when I call, I hear an undulating high-pitched whistle that makes my nose bleed.
jokes.setup: Describing problems contacting the IT lady
jokes.punchline: Increasingly supernatural and bizarre communication failures
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly escalating absurdity that makes the IT lady seem like an otherworldly entity

jokes.text: I'm assuming it's still the same, smaller holes, more bytes. / Now what are we up to now, mega? / Tera. / They did it, those bastards. They finally did it.
jokes.setup: Elroy showing his outdated tech knowledge
jokes.punchline: His dramatic reaction to learning about terabytes like it's apocalyptic
jokes.explanation: Perfect Elroy characterization - treating technological progress like a conspiracy against him

jokes.text: Can I be his assistant? / Not for money. / Can I be his friend? / Elroy Abed is your friend.
jokes.setup: Abed wanting to work with the new IT person
jokes.punchline: Descending expectations from paid work to just friendship
jokes.explanation: Shows Abed's desperation for tech involvement and Elroy's stilted but kind response

jokes.text: Now the real reason I came by, I'm dying. Not really, but imagine how bad you feel for complaining about the Wi-Fi.
jokes.setup: Dean dramatically announces he's dying
jokes.punchline: Immediately reveals it's fake to make them feel guilty
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection and shows Dean's manipulative tendencies in his quest for attention

jokes.text: Too short? It is too short? It's not too short? You people are no help.
jokes.setup: Dean asking for fashion advice on what appears to be an outfit
jokes.punchline: Getting no response and criticizing them for not helping with his mysterious concern
jokes.explanation: Classic Dean vanity moment, likely about one of his costumes or outfits

jokes.text: One, one more thing. You're gay, right? I mean, like openly gay.
jokes.setup: School board offering Dean a position
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly revealing they want him specifically because they think he's gay
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable moment that sets up the episode's main plot about tokenism and identity

jokes.text: I'm not openly anything, and gay doesn't begin to cover it.
jokes.setup: Being asked if he's openly gay
jokes.punchline: Dean's characteristically vague and over-complicated response about his sexuality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dean line - simultaneously evasive and revealing, hinting at his complex sexuality without defining it

jokes.text: Those guys get to dress fancy all year. We have one day!
jokes.setup: Explaining the controversy over cancelling pride parade for school board parade
jokes.punchline: Treating school board members like they're an oppressed group who deserve a parade
jokes.explanation: Good absurdity in treating bureaucrats like they need representation and celebration

jokes.text: Because, if you are that gayness could be a rocket, thrusting you to new gay heights. And if you're not. Well, there's a lot of gay guys out there.
jokes.setup: Trying to convince Dean to embrace being openly gay
jokes.punchline: Bizarre metaphor about gayness as propulsion system plus casual threat of replacement
jokes.explanation: Weird and memorable phrasing that shows the board member's clueless approach to diversity

jokes.text: I feel that's the selling point of the lifestyle. You know, I, I, I never thought about it that way. / Well, I have. Cutting women out of sex, it's genius.
jokes.setup: Dean considering the 'advantages' of gay lifestyle
jokes.punchline: Board member's incredibly crude and ignorant take on gay relationships
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable humor that shows the board member's awful understanding of sexuality

jokes.text: Gay dean. Gay dean. Gay dean. Gay dean.
jokes.setup: Trying to convince Dean to join the board
jokes.punchline: Chanting 'Gay dean' like a sports cheer
jokes.explanation: Absurd but somewhat cheap - relies on repetition more than clever writing

jokes.text: Same reason my mom took my half brother to Arizona.
jokes.setup: Explaining why the mother bird built nest near the router for heat
jokes.punchline: Casually referencing his family's dysfunction as parallel
jokes.explanation: Classic Abed - dropping casual references to family trauma while making nature observations

jokes.text: Kid, animals have been murdering each other for 3 billion years. Birds have had their 15 million in the spotlight.
jokes.setup: Abed worrying about the baby birds
jokes.punchline: Elroy's callous evolutionary perspective treating nature like show business
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Elroy characterization - combining scientific knowledge with entertainment industry cynicism

jokes.text: But we can't do any of it while scheduling our evolution around the needs of the least lucky birds.
jokes.setup: Continuing his argument about not accommodating the birds
jokes.punchline: Framing WiFi repair as evolutionary imperative vs bird welfare
jokes.explanation: Great Elroy voice - treating minor inconvenience as major philosophical question about progress vs nature

jokes.text: What are you, a demon? Did Clive Barker write you?
jokes.setup: Reacting to Elroy's callous bird murder monologue
jokes.punchline: Reference to horror writer as if Elroy is a fictional character
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed response - meta-textual reference that treats real person like fictional character he's analyzing

jokes.text: Mean? You just did a baby bird murder monologue.
jokes.setup: Elroy saying Abed doesn't have to get mean
jokes.punchline: Abed's deadpan summary of what just happened
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and character voice - Abed's matter-of-fact summary is funnier than elaboration

jokes.text: What's that? / Exactly.
jokes.setup: Asking what real IT people do to stall
jokes.punchline: Abed confirming that nobody knows what IT people actually do
jokes.explanation: Great observational humor about the mystery of IT work - setup and punchline work perfectly together

jokes.text: We were cast as Mr. Miagi and the Karate Kid, because we're a good team! / We're a team, because one of us is so talented she got cast outside her gender and the other one, got cast because of eye shape.
jokes.setup: Annie trying to be positive about their casting
jokes.punchline: Chang's brutal honesty about why they were each cast
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Annie's optimism and Chang's self-aware cynicism about racial casting

jokes.text: Let's say you're right. And like Sidney Poitier or Meg Ryan before you, you were cast for race.
jokes.setup: Annie trying to motivate Chang about racial casting
jokes.punchline: Including Meg Ryan as example of someone cast for their race
jokes.explanation: Annie's characteristic over-earnestness leads to absurd comparison

jokes.text: Wow, need time to fix it? You sound just like you work in IT.
jokes.setup: Elroy saying he needs time to fix the Wi-Fi problem
jokes.punchline: Jeff noting this matches the earlier joke about IT people stalling
jokes.explanation: Serviceable callback to earlier IT joke but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Well I got a part in a play and we were sort of talking about that, so read the room Pelton.
jokes.setup: Dean interrupting to talk about school board offer
jokes.punchline: Chang telling the Dean to read the room while being completely self-centered
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Chang's obliviousness to irony

jokes.text: They want you to be a token homosexual?
jokes.setup: Dean explaining the catch with the school board position
jokes.punchline: Jeff's blunt summary of the situation
jokes.explanation: Classic Jeff - cutting through euphemism to the uncomfortable truth

jokes.text: It's a form of progress, 30 years ago, the most power an openly gay could achieve was a center square.
jokes.setup: Discussing token representation
jokes.punchline: Reference to Paul Lynde on Hollywood Squares as peak gay representation
jokes.explanation: Sharp historical reference that makes a good point about representation progress

jokes.text: If coming out is a magic show, and gayness is the rabbit out of the hat, I'm one of those, never in any handkerchiefs.
jokes.setup: Dean trying to explain his complex sexuality
jokes.punchline: Convoluted magic metaphor that doesn't quite work
jokes.explanation: Pure Dean - overcomplicated metaphor that reveals more through its confusion than clarity

jokes.text: When a person becomes symbolic they gain symbolic power, at the price of independent power.
jokes.setup: Discussing Dean's dilemma about being a token
jokes.punchline: Jeff's surprisingly insightful analysis of political representation
jokes.explanation: Unexpectedly profound observation from Jeff that's both funny and true

jokes.text: And all I have to do is pare down my sexuality to simple gayness, which is heavily in the mix.
jokes.setup: Dean considering the political opportunity
jokes.punchline: Describing gayness as being 'heavily in the mix' of his sexuality
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dean characterization - treating his sexuality like a recipe with ingredients

jokes.text: Dean Pelton is coming out as approximately two-sevenths of what he is.
jokes.setup: Dean deciding to embrace partial honesty
jokes.punchline: Quantifying his coming out as a fraction
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Dean line that perfectly captures his mathematical approach to identity and partial disclosure

jokes.text: Quick adjustment, Ben. / Yeah? / Act better. Act better.
jokes.setup: Director stopping Chang's terrible performance
jokes.punchline: Giving him the most unhelpful acting direction possible
jokes.explanation: Great economy and captures the worst kind of directing - vague and unhelpful criticism

jokes.text: Keep me from puking is your motivation. Stop making me want to quit the theater and kill myself.
jokes.setup: Chang asking about motivation
jokes.punchline: Director's increasingly extreme responses to Chang's bad acting
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing the director's increasing desperation with Chang

jokes.text: I got distracted cuz the tools were doing a better job than you.
jokes.setup: Chang's performance continuing to be terrible
jokes.punchline: Director saying inanimate objects are better actors
jokes.explanation: Good insult with specific context to the scene they're performing

jokes.text: You crying? You cry when I tell you to cry. So reabsorb that disgusting droplet of salt and bad choices back into your doughy body.
jokes.setup: Chang starting to cry from the abuse
jokes.punchline: Director's incredibly specific and cruel description of tears
jokes.explanation: Memorably creative insult with vivid imagery - 'droplet of salt and bad choices' is brilliant

jokes.text: And then call your mother to see if you can be reabsorbed back into her doughy body or so help me God, I will take that tear, I will freeze it, and I will stab you in the eye with it, you waste of a soul-shaped hole forgotten by God.
jokes.setup: Director continuing his tirade against Chang
jokes.punchline: Escalating to frozen tear stabbing and existential insults
jokes.explanation: Incredibly creative and over-the-top insult that becomes almost poetic in its cruelty

jokes.text: and my partner, Domingo. / Very proud of my Craig.
jokes.setup: Dean introducing his fake boyfriend at press conference
jokes.punchline: Domingo playing the supportive partner role awkwardly
jokes.explanation: Sets up the awkwardness of Dean's fake relationship for political purposes

jokes.text: Which happens to be gay! Which is great, but irrelevant! / Hey, it gets better!
jokes.setup: School board trying to handle the gay question
jokes.punchline: Awkwardly shouting the 'It Gets Better' slogan
jokes.explanation: Shows the board's clumsy attempt to seem supportive while being tokenistic

jokes.text: I make gayness look like Mormonism.
jokes.setup: Chang accusing him of thinking gay people are scum
jokes.punchline: Dean's comparison to show how conservative his gayness is relative to his full sexuality
jokes.explanation: Brilliant line that perfectly captures Dean's sexual complexity in one comparison

jokes.text: Confine yourself to your role, Domingo. And lose the attitude.
jokes.setup: Domingo being supportive as fake boyfriend
jokes.punchline: Dean treating their fake relationship like a bad acting job
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing how Dean's deception is affecting relationships

jokes.text: I don't hit water, or old mayonnaise. Or the air after a fart has dissipated. And I'm not hitting you.
jokes.setup: Director explaining why he won't hit Chang
jokes.punchline: Increasingly disgusting comparisons to show Chang's irrelevance
jokes.explanation: Creative escalation of insults with vivid, gross imagery that builds to the punchline

jokes.text: You are the worst actor I've ever directed and I've directed both Wahlbergs!
jokes.setup: Director continuing his tirade against Chang
jokes.punchline: Reference to Mark and Donnie Wahlberg's acting abilities
jokes.explanation: Good celebrity reference that works as an insult while being topical

jokes.text: Danny does a bit with the floor sanders, Miyagi is amused, and Kenny gets me another god damned cup of coffee with six Splendas in it!
jokes.setup: Director giving scene direction
jokes.punchline: Escalating to yelling at his assistant about coffee specifics
jokes.explanation: Shows the director's control-freak nature extending to minor details

jokes.text: I was kind of born to act, Britta. When I do it, I can feel it pleasing the universe.
jokes.setup: Annie explaining why she shouldn't quit
jokes.punchline: Grandiose claim about her cosmic acting purpose
jokes.explanation: Perfect Annie line - combining narcissism with pseudo-spiritual grandiosity

jokes.text: That's dramatic. / See?
jokes.setup: Britta commenting on Annie's dramatic statement
jokes.punchline: Annie treating this as proof of her acting ability
jokes.explanation: Great economy - Annie turning criticism into validation in two words

jokes.text: If they're albatrosses, we're in trouble. Albatrosses have a 270 day fledgling period.
jokes.setup: Discussing how long until the birds can leave
jokes.punchline: Abed's specific knowledge creating worst-case scenario
jokes.explanation: Classic Abed - having oddly specific knowledge that makes situation worse

jokes.text: Well of course not. If you move them the mother won't come back. Keep up the good work.
jokes.setup: Elroy explaining about the bird situation
jokes.punchline: Frankie's complete 180 when she understands
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Frankie's reasonableness once she has facts

jokes.text: you make me embarrassed to have thumbs, I can see air quotes around you.
jokes.setup: Director continuing his tirade
jokes.punchline: Abstract insults about thumbs and visible air quotes
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd insults - 'embarrassed to have thumbs' is memorably weird

jokes.text: Lead? You played Danny LaRusso. / Well I'm the Karate Kid. / The Karate Kid is about Kesuke Miyagi.
jokes.setup: Annie thinking she's the lead character
jokes.punchline: Director explaining the movie is actually about Mr. Miyagi
jokes.explanation: Good point about the actual themes of Karate Kid that deflates Annie's ego

jokes.text: Ralph Macchio? Showed up.
jokes.setup: Comparing Miyagi's Oscar nomination to Danny's role
jokes.punchline: Dismissing Ralph Macchio's contribution in two words
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and captures the director's harsh but accurate assessment

jokes.text: I cast you because your measurements allow me to use the same wardrobe as last year.
jokes.setup: Annie thinking she was cast for talent
jokes.punchline: Director revealing she was cast for purely practical costume reasons
jokes.explanation: Brutal deflation of Annie's ego with mundane practical reasoning

jokes.text: I cast Ben because he has the sadness and talent that could make this show great. If I have to physically drill into his chest and suck it out with a straw!
jokes.setup: Explaining why he cast Chang
jokes.punchline: Violent metaphor for extracting Chang's potential
jokes.explanation: Dark and creative metaphor that shows the director's obsessive vision

jokes.text: So you can take a flying kick and a rolling donut! You're fired.
jokes.setup: Director firing Annie
jokes.punchline: Mixing karate move with pastry for dismissive send-off
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay but feels somewhat forced compared to his other insults

jokes.text: Oh my God! You're like doing a terrible Vinnie Barbarino up here. But, like, I know you're not capable of anything better, so, that's why I've been so nice to you.
jokes.setup: Annie asking if she's talented too
jokes.punchline: Director explaining his 'kindness' was actually condescension
jokes.explanation: Good revelation that recontextualizes all his previous 'nice' treatment of Annie

jokes.text: That's a little too gay. / I didn't do it because I'm gay. Wait, I'm not gay.
jokes.setup: Board criticizing Dean's bird protection
jokes.punchline: Dean's confused stumbling over his fake identity
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dean's confusion about his fake identity

jokes.text: You know why? Because the whole point is to get a job by being gay and then to do as good of job as the normals.
jokes.setup: Explaining why gay people aren't supporting Dean
jokes.punchline: Crude explanation of tokenism expectations
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable but accurate observation about tokenism dynamics

jokes.text: They don't want a gay dean who acts like a weird gay monster.
jokes.setup: Continuing criticism of Dean's behavior
jokes.punchline: Describing Dean as a 'weird gay monster'
jokes.explanation: Memorable phrase that captures the board's frustration with Dean's behavior

jokes.text: This is two white security guards versus one unarmed black man and two baby birds. Your move.
jokes.setup: Security confronting Elroy about the bird nest
jokes.punchline: Framing the situation in terms that make security look bad
jokes.explanation: Smart tactical move by Elroy, reframing the optics to his advantage

jokes.text: Just one unarmed black man versus one unarmed, openly gay dean.
jokes.setup: Dean taking over the confrontation
jokes.punchline: Reframing it as a battle of minorities
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Elroy's tactic, showing Dean learning political maneuvering

jokes.text: I belong to one of the most marginalized and least openly honest groups in America. I am a politician.
jokes.setup: Dean's press conference about being dishonest
jokes.punchline: Revealing his true identity as a politician instead of gay man
jokes.explanation: Brilliant reversal that subverts expectations and makes a sharp point about political honesty

jokes.text: We like our politicians in the closest because we're afraid, deep down, we're all a little political.
jokes.setup: Explaining why politicians being political is scandalous
jokes.punchline: Playing on 'closet' metaphor and universal political nature
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on closet metaphor that makes a good point about political identity

jokes.text: Two of them died. There's one left. He's hanging on, but I'm worried he's turning into a symbol of my own innocence.
jokes.setup: Abed reporting on the bird situation
jokes.punchline: Treating the bird as a metaphor for his moral state
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed line - overthinking a simple situation into philosophical territory

jokes.text: If any of you go, you're supporting abuse. / Oh, dial it back, Annie.
jokes.setup: Annie being dramatic about Chang's play
jokes.punchline: Jeff's dismissive response to her overreaction
jokes.explanation: Standard character interaction showing Annie's tendency to dramatize

jokes.text: It'll be interesting to see how his fake crying is different from his real crying.
jokes.setup: Deciding to go to Chang's play
jokes.punchline: Expecting Chang's acting to be indistinguishable from reality
jokes.explanation: Good character-based humor about Chang's acting ability and emotional authenticity

jokes.text: Well I'm a big fan of the performing arts, but I'll go to Chang's play anyway.
jokes.setup: Britta deciding to attend Chang's play
jokes.punchline: Implying Chang's play doesn't qualify as performing arts
jokes.explanation: Good Britta line showing her pretentious attitude while still being supportive

jokes.text: Actors are worthless, empty-headed homunculi, but the right one, in the right role can change the world.
jokes.setup: Director giving philosophy before the show
jokes.punchline: Contradictory view of actors as both worthless and world-changing
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing director's complex relationship with actors

jokes.text: He replaced me with Annie Kim.
jokes.setup: Seeing who replaced her in the play
jokes.punchline: It's Annie Kim, her academic rival
jokes.explanation: Good callback to established character rivalry that adds insult to injury

jokes.text: Oh, shit.
jokes.setup: Chang delivering emotional performance about his wife losing child
jokes.punchline: Audience member's shocked reaction to Chang's surprising acting ability
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reaction showing audience surprise at Chang's actual talent

jokes.text: I'm speechless. / I forgot Chang was up there. / Me, too.
jokes.setup: Group watching Chang's surprisingly good performance
jokes.punchline: Forgetting it's Chang because he's actually acting well
jokes.explanation: Good payoff showing how Chang's performance surprised everyone who knows him

jokes.text: Maybe that's the lesson. / Maybe we all lost a button.
jokes.setup: Abed saying he's at a loss about what lesson to learn
jokes.punchline: Elroy's non-sequitur about losing buttons
jokes.explanation: Classic Community ending - avoiding clear lessons with absurdist observations

jokes.text: Believe me, I sit in my dressing room and all I dream about is a white picket fence and a dog. / I don't have those either.
jokes.setup: Chang talking about wanting normal life after acting success
jokes.punchline: Jeff's deadpan response that he also lacks these basic things
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing both characters' lack of conventional stability

jokes.text: I know this great bar for people that don't hate themselves. / What do you say?
jokes.setup: Jeff suggesting they go out
jokes.punchline: Implication that their usual places are for self-hating people
jokes.explanation: Good character moment revealing their typical hangout preferences

jokes.text: Oh, I'll always remember how you kept me from getting email for a week. And that chirp you do when you want more bread.
jokes.setup: Bird funeral speech
jokes.punchline: Treating the bird like a pet with specific behaviors
jokes.explanation: Classic Abed - anthropomorphizing the bird with specific relationship details

jokes.text: And now everyone, please put on your cat masks and finger wings. With these cat masks, we remind you that this place can no longer be your home, but with the fluttering of these wings, we symbolically join your flight into the natural world.
jokes.setup: Abed conducting elaborate bird funeral
jokes.punchline: Overly complex ceremony with cat masks and finger wings
jokes.explanation: Perfect Abed - creating an unnecessarily elaborate ritual for a simple situation

jokes.text: Thanks to you, that bird has his whole bird life ahead of him, to fly, meet lady birds, even start a bird family. You made a difference. / Aw. / Abed.
jokes.setup: Britta giving heartfelt speech about the bird's future
jokes.punchline: Bird immediately dies after being released
jokes.explanation: Classic dark comedy ending - undermining the feel-good moment immediately
